response.overview: In Istefan, on the ancient shores of the Black Sea, time is tightening. Musician Şerafettin, who returned to his village for peace, is shaken by the roar of dynamite; the walls of the house he built with his own hands are cracking . The seven-day cycle of the boat Hızır7—the film’s namesake—captures a struggle for survival, the people's resistance against illegal fish farms, and an ecosystem’s silent cry . In this narrative, edited by nature’s own raw rhythm, the tension between man, nature, and capital transforms into an existential story: "We came from nature, and to nature we shall return.
response.title: Hızır7Gün
